questions.question: What is total surface area (TSA) of a solid?
questions.correct_answer: Sum of the areas of all surfaces of the solid
questions.explanation: Includes all faces for cuboid/cube and curved + base areas for cylinders, cones, and spheres.

questions.question: What is curved surface area (CSA)?
questions.correct_answer: Area of only the curved surface of a solid
questions.explanation: Excludes base(s) in solids like cylinders and cones.

questions.question: Formula for TSA of a cuboid?
questions.explanation: Sum of areas of all six rectangular faces, where l = length, b = breadth, h = height.

questions.question: Formula for TSA of a cube?
questions.explanation: All six faces are squares with side a.

questions.question: Formula for CSA of a cylinder?
questions.explanation: Area of the curved lateral surface only, excluding top and bottom.

questions.question: Formula for TSA of a cylinder?
questions.explanation: Sum of curved surface area 2πrh and areas of two bases 2πr².

questions.question: Formula for volume of a cuboid?
questions.explanation: Product of length, breadth, and height.

questions.question: Formula for volume of a cube?
Formula for volume of a cylinder?
Formula for volume of a cone?

notes.title: Surface Areas
notes.content: TSA includes all surfaces; CSA includes only curved surfaces excluding bases.

notes.title: Cuboid & Cube
notes.content: Formulas depend on side lengths; cube is a special cuboid with all sides equal.

notes.title: Cone
notes.content: CSA = πrl, TSA = πr(l + r), Volume = (1/3)πr²h, l = slant height.

facts.fact: TSA includes all faces or surfaces of a solid.
CSA excludes the base(s) of a solid.
Volume measures the space enclosed by a solid.
Cube is a special cuboid with all sides equal.
Volume of cone = 1/3 × base area × height.
